page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz, the instance features 2 physical cores with a single thread per core. The processor architecture is x86_64, supported by a KVM hypervisor. It includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The hardware configuration lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng CPU test of 1842.18 and Geekbench single-core score of 1556.0, place the instance in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1932.39 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1815.0, both falling into the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth also registers on the lower end, with large-block read and write speeds of 15.1 GB/sec and 13.63 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic and compression tasks show moderate capability, as evidenced by a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 27.62 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU encryption score of 1820.51.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g7se.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation at an entry-level resource scale, making it a predictable option for workloads that do not require high multi-threaded density or local storage. Given the moderate single-core performance and lower multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads. These include basic web serving, single-threaded application testing, lightweight text processing, and small-scale database operations that do not demand high IOPS or extensive parallel processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.9 GHz. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1556.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 27.62 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 1815.0 and large-block read bandwidth of 15.1 GB/sec. Qualitatively, the instance offers dedicated compute resources at a low-density scale, making it suitable for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, text processing, and single-threaded application tasks.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with balanced memory configuration for lightweight, single-threaded applications and basic web serving.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
